summary: Matched a substantial block of All-Star mode game-mode code in src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c, replacing several placeholder functions with C implementations and moving/reifying All-Star opponent/round data. The PR introduced static gm_803DEBE8 opponent data, a new gm_803DEC4C AllstarRoundInfo table, and implementations for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC. It also adjusted GALE01 symbol sizes to split gm_803DEBE8 from the newly identified gm_803DEC4C object. The author explicitly described the result as an imperfect base for others to improve, and there was no substantive review feedback in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added includes for gmregcommon.h and melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h to support enemy color setup and audio-preload calls from the newly decompiled All-Star functions.

Replaced placeholder marker for gm_801B5324 with an implementation that prepares All-Star opponent character/color arrays, configures enemy color data, fills lbDvd preload cache entries, sets stage cache data, and accumulates audio preload bitmasks for the player, opponents, and stage.

Replaced placeholder marker for gm_801B5624 with an implementation that builds StartMeleeData for an All-Star match, derives opponent data from the current minor-scene round, configures rules and player state, handles special final-round setup for idx 0x60, and stores progress back to global All-Star data.

Replaced placeholder marker for gm_801B59AC with an implementation that records match result state in gm_80490940, preserves player percent and elapsed frame count into All-Star data, and conditionally advances scene flow through gm_8017CBAC when appropriate.

Replaced placeholder marker for gm_801B5ACC with an implementation for All-Star between-round/rest-area setup, including rule flags, opponent tracking arrays, next-round preview data, random placement into allstar->x76, calls to gm_8016A92C/gm_8016A998, preloading the next round via gm_801B5324, and assigning fn_801B5AA8 as a rules callback.
Defined static gm_803DEBE8[25] opponent data directly in gmallstar.c instead of keeping it as an extern plus commented initializer in gmallstar.static.h.
Introduced static AllstarRoundInfo gm_803DEC4C[26] in gmallstar.c, with start/count pairs used to map All-Star rounds to slices of the opponent table.
Added typedef struct AllstarRoundInfo with u32 start and u32 count fields to gmallstar.static.h.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so gm_803DEBE8 shrank from size 0xD0 to 0x64 and a new gm_803DEC4C object at .data:0x803DEC4C size 0x6C was added.

Touched gm_801B60A4_OnLoad by removing a PAD_STACK(12) and changing a byte-zeroing loop over gm_80490940 from pointer post-increment form to an indexed for loop, presumably to support matching after nearby data/function changes.
smart_moves: Split the old 0xD0 data symbol into two typed objects: gm_803DEBE8 for the 25-entry opponent table and gm_803DEC4C for round start/count metadata. This makes later All-Star work more searchable and less dependent on raw offsets.
Moved the large gm_803DEBE8 initializer out of a comment in the static header and into the C file as a real static data definition, reducing header clutter and aligning storage with local data scope.
Introduced AllstarRoundInfo rather than indexing round metadata as raw words, making repeated uses of .start and .count explicit in the new function bodies.

Used existing game-mode helper functions rather than open-coding systems already present elsewhere: gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable for colors, lbDvd cache functions for preload, lbAudioAx functions for audio masks, gm_8017CE34 for melee-data setup, and gm_8016F088/gm_8016A92C/gm_8016A998 for rules/progress hooks.

Preserved several compiler-shape artifacts likely needed for matching, including small inner scopes, explicit casts between u8/s8/s32, manual unrolled copies in blocks of eight, and PAD_STACK calls in newly implemented functions.
decomp_lessons: When a previously monolithic data symbol does not match real usage, split it into adjacent typed objects and update symbols.txt sizes accordingly; here gm_803DEBE8 became a 0x64 opponent table and gm_803DEC4C became a separate 0x6C round-info table.
For game-mode decomp, reconstructing data tables can unlock multiple functions at once because scene setup, preload, result handling, and between-round flow all index the same tables.

Keep uncertain, address-style names when semantics are not proven. This PR improved structure around gm_803DEBE8 and gm_803DEC4C but retained many xN field names and gm_801xxxxx function names, which is appropriate when evidence is limited.

Matching may require code that looks unnatural in source form, such as manual block-copy loops, redundant round recomputation, temporary base pointers to minor-scene data, explicit casts, and stack padding.
Header hygiene matters: static local data definitions should not remain as extern declarations or commented-out initializers in a static header once the owning C file defines them.

Game-mode functions often need coordinated preloading of character files, stage files, and audio banks; future matching should look for lbDvd and lbAudioAx call sequences together rather than treating them as unrelated.
naming_conventions: New table type uses a semantic PascalCase typedef name: AllstarRoundInfo.
AllstarRoundInfo fields use concise semantic names start and count rather than raw x0/x4 fields.
Unresolved objects/functions retain address-based names such as gm_803DEBE8, gm_803DEC4C, gm_80490940, gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC.
Unresolved struct fields retain x-offset/bitfield-style names such as x0, x2, x3, x54, x74, x9C, x0_6, x0_7, x1_0, xC_b1, and xD_b2.

Local variable names in the new implementations are partly semantic and reusable for similar work: opp_data, is_last_round, count_processed, slot_idx, allstar, round, color, opp_start, opp_idx, match_end, next_count.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in several newly implemented functions, with sizes visible in the diff such as PAD_STACK(12), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(24), to align stack layout for matching.
Kept explicit s8/u8 casts around character IDs and color values, especially when writing chars arrays and calling lbAudioAx_80026E84 or assigning preload cache char_id fields.
Implemented copy loops with an eight-element unrolled body and a residual loop, likely matching compiler output for copying opponent character IDs from gm_803DEBE8_t.x3 into local chars arrays.
Used local inner scopes to control temporary lifetimes and register allocation around start/count table lookups and later loops.
Preserved apparently redundant computations such as recomputing round with gm_8017BE84(arg0->idx) before separate uses, which can be necessary for instruction scheduling/register matching.

Adjusted gm_801B60A4_OnLoad stack/zeroing behavior by removing PAD_STACK(12) and changing the gm_80490940 byte-clear loop shape, indicating small surrounding edits were needed to keep the function matching.

review_feedback.feedback: The author wrote that they were "Not the biggest fan on how this one turned out" and framed it as "a base for others to work off of for now." This indicates the implementation was considered useful but not final polish.
No review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c to see whether gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, or gm_801B5ACC were cleaned up or renamed.
Search for later renames of gm_803DEBE8_t, gm_803DEC4C, AllstarRoundInfo, and gm_80490940 to recover final semantic names.
Inspect adjacent All-Star minor-scene tables in gm_803DE930_MinorScenes to confirm whether the round index and idx 0x60/final-round handling were later documented.
Compare other game-mode files using gm_8017CE34 and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable for reusable patterns in StartMeleeData setup and enemy color table reconstruction.
Audit symbols.txt changes around .data:0x803DEBE8-.data:0x803DECB8 to confirm the object split remained stable after later data matching.
